title: APPENDIX — Quick-reference summary (not for reading aloud)

lines.text: APPENDIX — Quick-reference summary (not for reading aloud)
Target 1 — Cell wall (largest selective-toxicity gap → safest drugs)
Beta-lactams (penicillins, cephalosporins, carbapenems, monobactams): inactivate transpeptidases (PBPs); beta-lactam ring mimics D-Ala-D-Ala; bactericidal; time-dependent.
Resistance: beta-lactamase (destroys drug), PBP2a/mecA = MRSA (target change).
Vancomycin (glycopeptide): binds D-Ala-D-Ala substrate; bactericidal; gram-positive only (too large for gram-negative outer membrane).
Resistance: D-Ala-D-Lac = VRE.
Others: fosfomycin (early synthesis), bacitracin (lipid carrier recycling), cycloserine (D-Ala-D-Ala synthesis).
Target 2 — Ribosome (overlap with mitochondrial 70S-like ribosomes → toxicity)
30S: aminoglycosides (misreading + initiation block; bactericidal; concentration-dependent; long PAE; O2-dependent uptake so no anaerobes; nephro/ototoxic).
Tetracyclines (block A-site tRNA docking; static; chelate cations).
50S: macrolides (exit tunnel/translocation; static; P450 inhibitors).
Chloramphenicol (peptidyl transferase; static; marrow suppression).
Clindamycin (elongation; anaerobes; C. diff).
Linezolid (blocks initiation complex; unique site, no cross-resistance).
MLS-B resistance via erm methylation hits macrolides + lincosamides + streptogramins together.
Target 3 — Nucleic acids
Fluoroquinolones: trap DNA gyrase/topo IV mid-cleavage → double-strand breaks; bactericidal; concentration-dependent; tendon/QT/cartilage cautions.
Rifampin: inhibits RNA polymerase (rpoB); bactericidal; single-mutation resistance → never monotherapy; P450 inducer; orange fluids.
Metronidazole: prodrug activated only in anaerobes/protozoa → DNA damage; alcohol reaction.
Target 4 — Folate synthesis (pathway absent in humans → very selective)
Sulfonamides: PABA mimic, inhibit dihydropteroate synthase.
Trimethoprim: inhibits bacterial dihydrofolate reductase (selective affinity vs human enzyme).
Combination = sequential blockade = synergistic, bactericidal.
Target 5 — Membrane (smallest gap → most toxic, last-resort)
Daptomycin: inserts into gram-positive membrane (Ca-dependent), depolarizes; bactericidal; inactivated by lung surfactant (no pneumonia use).
Polymyxins/colistin: disrupt gram-negative membranes; bactericidal; nephro/neurotoxic; last resort.
Four resistance strategies: (1) inactivate/destroy drug; (2) modify target; (3) reduce entry (porin loss); (4) efflux pumps.
Spread by plasmids (horizontal transfer) under antibiotic selection pressure.
Two organizing principles: (1) selective-toxicity gap predicts safety; (2) mechanism of action predicts mechanism of resistance.
